_doc: Employer name aliases + collision exclusions for unified employer search. Loaded by api/routers/employers.py::_load_aliases() and applied as WHERE LOWER(search_name) NOT LIKE %s%% guards when the user's query matches one of an entry's aliases. R7-7 (2026-04-28). To add a new entry: pick a canonical_name (display only), list lowercase aliases the user might type, list lowercase exclude_terms that should NOT appear in results when those aliases match. Fail-open if this file is missing or malformed.

aliases.notes: Trigram similarity ranks Cleveland-Cliffs Inc (steel/mining) high on 'cleveland clinic' queries because of the shared prefix. Distinct entities.

aliases.canonical_name: New York City Health and Hospitals Corporation
aliases.notes: NYC public hospital system; NYU Langone is a separate private system. Audit cited NYC Hospitals query returning NYU Langone.

Multi-state Blue Cross plan. The acronym 'HCSC' is a known organizer-target lookup; the trigram score for 'hcsc' against the long form is low so users often miss it. No exclude_terms today; entry kept for documentation and future expansion.
Trigram + new tiebreak (canonical_group_id + consolidated_workers) ranks Walmart Inc parent above per-store fragments. Entry kept for documentation; no exclude_terms needed.
Same as Walmart -- tiebreak handles Amazon parent vs warehouse fragments. Entry kept for documentation.
